_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron (Adam Johns)
_meta.note: Federal AZ-01 open-seat batch — Schweikert pivot to Gov leaves AZ-01 OPEN with crowded primary July 21 2026. R primary: Chaplik (AZ Freedom Caucus founder/VC, 95+ purity), Feely (Trump-endorsed, scored prior), Trobough (scored prior). D primary: Treble (cash leader, $1.7M COH, JACPAC pro-Israel deviation), Galán-Woods (DCCC Red-to-Blue, EMILY's List, anti-defund deviation), Gordon (voting-rights attorney, Jewish/LGBTQ DNC, pro-Ukraine HARD). Ugenti-Rita NEVER FILED for AZ-01 — withdrew, endorsed Chaplik, pivoted to Scottsdale City Council 2026.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (2026 D primary cash leader $2.48M raised / $1.67M COH 3/31; open Schweikert seat; primary July 21 2026)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — federal evidence sweep on AZ-01 open-seat D cash leader. Generic progressive platform (Medicare-for-All, codify Roe, Voting Rights Act restoration, no fossil-fuel money pledge, restore Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund, anti-Trump). One CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: JACPAC pro-Israel PAC endorsement + Newsweek quote framing 'hostages and their families' alongside Gaza civilians — places him in pro-Israel-Dem camp, NOT progressive ceasefire-now wing. 'Get ICE off our streets' TV ad pushed by NRCC. NO documented positions on guns, marriage/sexuality, RL, Ukraine, China, Iran, foreign aid — all NULL per evidence-first discipline. JACPAC endorsement triggers foreign_policy_restraint q3 FALSE (foreign-linked PAC).
profile.religion: Unknown — no public statement of faith located on campaign site, Wikipedia, or Ballotpedia (JACPAC endorsement is suggestive of Jewish-community ties but JACPAC endorses non-Jewish allies; insufficient evidence)
profile.background: Founder/CEO WithMe (smart-amenities tech company, 100+ employees; originally launched PrintWithMe public-printer network for apartments). Wharton School BS 2010; raised in Jersey City NJ. Married, father of young daughter. Near-fatal vascular brain growth requiring $2M surgery — origin story for Medicare-for-All push. Campaign launched 2025; FEC H6AZ01298. 2026-03-31: $2.48M raised, $1.67M COH — D primary leader.

jonathan-treble.notes_set: AZ-01 OPEN-SEAT D PRIMARY CASH LEADER (Schweikert pivoted to Gov). Cited FALSEs (anti-rubric HARD): pro-choice 'decisions about pregnancy should be made by patients and their doctors. Not politicians in DC'; supports restoring Roe + abortion/contraception/IVF access (sanctity q0); pro-Title-I/IDEA/Pell expansion + opposes fed school-cuts (family q0); pro-spending (Medicare-for-All + universal childcare + restored Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund — econ q2); 'restore the Voting Rights Act' + 'expand early voting and mail-in ballot access' (election q3); 'get ICE off our streets' TV ad + 'modernizing legal immigration, protecting Dreamers' (border q0, q1, q2); 'no fossil fuels money pledge'; tariffs 'a tax on everyday Americans' anti-Trump-tariff. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (cited TRUE): JACPAC pro-Israel PAC endorsement — JACPAC is pro-Israel two-state-aligned political PAC, qualifies as foreign-linked-lobby donation per rubric (foreign q3 FALSE); same endorsement maps to foreign q2 NOT-FALSE (Israel is ally not hostile nation — q2 NULL). NULL on marriage, christian_liberty, gun-rights, Ukraine, China, Iran, NFA/NCVIA pharma rubric — no campaign-site statements located; do NOT infer from party. Confidence_note flags the sparse-data trap. Religion unknown.

0.note: Campaign Issues page: 'Decisions about pregnancy should be made by patients and their doctors. Not politicians in DC' — supports restoring Roe + contraception/IVF access; does NOT affirm life-from-conception
Supports 'fully fund[ing] Title I and II and IDEA, expand[ing] Pell Grants' — pro federal public-school funding posture, no school-choice/homeschool freedom support found

2.note: Supports Medicare-for-All + universal childcare + restoring Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund + fully-funded Title I/II/IDEA — pro-spending posture, no balanced-budget support

3.note: Campaign Issues page: 'expand early voting and mail-in ballot access' + 'champion restoring the Voting Rights Act' — directly opposes oppose-mass-mail-in posture

0.note: NRCC documented TV ad: 'get ICE off our streets'; campaign page calls for 'humane process' over 'chaos and cruelty of federal policy' — no support for wall completion or military border presence

1.note: Issues page: 'protecting Dreamers' + 'modernizing legal immigration' — supports DACA/Dreamer status, contradicts mandatory-deportation rubric position

2.note: 'Get ICE off our streets' TV ad — directly contradicts federal-preemption-against-sanctuary-cities position

3.note: JACPAC (Joint Action Committee for Political Affairs) pro-Israel PAC endorsement listed on Wikipedia entry — qualifies as foreign-linked-lobby PAC donation per rubric (q3 FALSE — accepted donation from foreign-linked PAC)

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (2026 R primary — AZ Freedom Caucus founder/VC; resigned AZ House March 2026 to focus on congressional bid; open Schweikert seat; primary July 21 2026)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — federal evidence sweep on AZ-01 open-seat R candidate. AZ Freedom Caucus founder + Vice Chair with DEEP state-house voting record (Jan 2021 – March 2026, LD-23 then LD-3) — HARD evidence dominates. CAP 100%, Secular Coalition AZ 0%, Club for Growth 100, ACU 100, Free Enterprise Club 100, Taxpayers Protection Alliance 98 lifetime, Republican Liberty Caucus 104, NRA-A. Phoenix New Times labeled him 'the most far-right candidate in the race.' Voted NO on HB2677 (would have repealed the 1864 AZ abortion ban) — MORE pro-life than the median AZ R. Trump declined to endorse him (endorsed Feely + Swoboda; Swoboda later withdrew). NO documented positions located on Israel/Ukraine/Iran/China/Trade/Foreign-Aid — declined PBS debate where Iran was top topic, did not answer iVoterGuide on those — all NULL (silence-by-omission, not deviation). Catholic; parishioner at St. Patrick's Catholic Church (Scottsdale).
profile.religion: Catholic (parishioner at St. Patrick's Catholic Church, Scottsdale AZ)
profile.background: Founder, Joseph Bernard Investment Real Estate (commercial/multi-family RE firm w/ offices in 3 states; 25+ years executive leadership, previously Fortune-500 telecom). BA Bowling Green State University 1993. AZ House LD-23 (Jan 11 2021 – Jan 2023) then LD-3 post-redistricting (Jan 2023 – March 2 2026 — resigned to focus on AZ-01 bid). Founding member & Vice Chair, Arizona Freedom Caucus. Chaired House Ethics Committee; Vice Chair Appropriations + Commerce. 2023 R candidate for Speaker of the House (lost). Married, two sons; Scottsdale resident. Filed CD-1 candidacy Dec 15 2025.

joseph-chaplik.notes_set: AZ-01 OPEN-SEAT R PRIMARY CANDIDATE — AZ Freedom Caucus founder/VC, deep state-house record (HARD). Cited TRUEs across rubric: sanctity_of_life q0 (Catholic, 'pro-life' bio + voted YES on AZ SB1164 15-wk ban, SB1457 genetic-abnormality ban, HB2811 criminalize abortion meds, HB2547 defund providers, HB2681 in-person exam, SB1600/SCR1009 born-alive; voted NO on HB2677 repealing 1864 ban; CAP 100%); sanctity q4 (no PP/NARAL/EMILY's funding — Secular Coalition AZ 0%, CAP 100%); biblical_marriage q2 (sponsored pronoun-usage restrictions in schools, opposed gender-reassignment for minors); biblical_marriage q4 (Secular Coalition AZ 0% on LGBTQ-promotion legislation); family q0 ('Extended K-12 ESA to all Arizona families'); family q1 (sponsored HB2616 parental consent for masks; HB2458 classroom material publication); family q2 (sponsored pronoun-usage restrictions, classroom material publication HB2699/HB2458/HB2539); christian_liberty q1 (sponsored clergy-visitation-during-emergencies + religious vaccine-mandate exemptions); economic_stewardship q2 (Club for Growth 100, Taxpayers Protection Alliance 98 lifetime, 'battle-tested to fight reckless spending'); economic_stewardship q4 (Republican Liberty Caucus 104 anti-cronyism, Free Enterprise Club 100); election_integrity q0 (40 election bills incl. hand-counted-ballot legislation per record page); election_integrity q1 (sponsored HB2617 voter-roll cleanup — Ducey vetoed); election_integrity q3 (mail-in voting restrictions — sponsored); border q0 (sponsored bills 'funded border wall'); border q2 (sponsored bills 'establishing criminal liability for unlawful immigration'); self_defense q0 (sponsored HB2316/HB2414/HB2472/HB2473/SB1220/HB2111/HB2551/HB2827 concealed-carry expansion); self_defense q1 (NRA A-rated, endorsed by Gun Owners of America + National Assoc for Gun Rights — opposes AWB/UBC/red-flag); industry_capture q0 (HB2294, HB2633 anti-vaccine-mandate, anti-mask-mandate; Stand For Health Freedom endorsement); industry_capture q4 (general deregulation posture — SOFT). NULL on Israel/Ukraine/Iran/China/AIPAC/foreign-aid/trade — declined PBS debate where Iran was top topic; did not answer iVoterGuide questionnaire on these. NO cross-party deviations found.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (2026 D primary — voting-rights attorney; open Schweikert seat; primary July 21 2026)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — federal evidence sweep on AZ-01 open-seat D candidate. Campaign has NO Issues page (404 on /issues) — bio + DNC role + career-as-position. HARD positions documented: pro-Ukraine (3 personal humanitarian missions to Ukraine + Ukraine Focus Advisory Committee member); pro-voting-access (30+ year voting-rights attorney, Founder/Chair Emeritus Voting Rights Fund PAC, MCDP Voting Rights Director); anti-Trump ('wannabe dictator' + Schweikert 'henchman'). Jewish; DNC LGBTQ+ Caucus member; LGBTQ pro-bono advocacy. NO documented positions on 17 of 22 issues — most cells NULL per evidence-first discipline. Ran for AZ Secretary of State 2018 (lost D primary).
profile.religion: Jewish (parents 'first-generation Jewish-Americans'; pro-bono work for 'Jewish agencies'; co-produced Off-Broadway play '2 1/2 Jews')
profile.background: Attorney — election protection / voting rights / civil rights / entertainment law; 30+ years. Bar admissions: US Supreme Court, US District Court (NJ), DC, NJ, NY. Operates MRG Law Office (Washington DC). Founder/Chair Emeritus Voting Rights Fund PAC. 30-yr SAG-AFTRA member (professional actor/director/writer/producer). Princeton (BA) → Columbia Law (JD) → Harvard Kennedy School. Phoenix native (Osborn Elementary, Phoenix Union HS). Father = disabled WWII veteran; mother = public school teacher. AZ DNC Committeeman; DNC Small Business Council Chair; DNC Labor/Disability/LGBTQ+/Veterans councils. Prior run: AZ Secretary of State 2018 (lost D primary). FEC C00911404.

mark-robert-gordon.notes_set: AZ-01 OPEN-SEAT D PRIMARY CANDIDATE — voting-rights attorney w/ thin issue platform (no campaign Issues page). Cited FALSEs (HARD): election_integrity q1 (30+ yr voting-rights attorney, Founder/Chair Emeritus Voting Rights Fund PAC, MCDP Voting Rights Director — career posture is anti voter-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions); election q3 (career posture = pro mail-in / pro voting-access expansion); foreign_policy q1 (3 personal humanitarian missions to Ukraine + Ukraine Focus Advisory Committee — actively supports continued US engagement in Ukraine, opposes withdrawal/AUMF-repeal restraint posture). NULL on 17 issues incl. abortion, marriage/Equality-Act (DNC LGBTQ+ Caucus + AFL-CIO Pride Month profile = SOFT signal only, no explicit Equality Act statement), gun rights, religious liberty, parental rights, immigration, Israel (notable absence given Jewish heritage + DNC LGBTQ+ role — has NOT publicly aligned ceasefire-now/Hamas-sympathetic OR AIPAC), China, Iran, debt, energy, climate, police, foreign aid, tariffs, healthcare specifics (M4A/public-option silent — defends ACA/Medicare/Soc-Sec generically), tax specifics, regulation, industry capture. NO cross-party deviations found. Religion: Jewish.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (2026 D primary — DCCC Red-to-Blue + EMILY's List Majority Maker; 2024 D primary 3rd place; widow of fmr AZ AG Grant Woods; open Schweikert seat; primary July 21 2026)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — federal evidence sweep on AZ-01 open-seat D candidate. 2024 AZ-01 primary 3rd place finisher; relaunched March 2025. DCCC Red-to-Blue (May 2025); EMILY's List 'Majority Maker'; Bold PAC, Elect Democratic Women, Alliance for Retired Americans, J Street PAC, AZ AG Kris Mayes, fmr AZ Gov Janet Napolitano endorsements. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: 'Strongly opposes any calls to defund the police' (HARD pro-LEO — federal rubric has no police cell, so deviation noted but doesn't flip a cell); 'secure borders' rhetoric paired w/ Dreamer-amnesty (SOFT moderate-D); anti-tariff posture; ACA-incrementalist not M4A. Widow of Grant Woods (fmr AZ AG R-turned-D, McCain Republican, endorsed Biden 2020 — Marlene herself former R who switched citing Trump). Cuban-American first-gen; former TV broadcast journalist. J Street PAC endorsement = pro-Israel two-state lobby donation (foreign q3 FALSE — foreign-linked PAC).
profile.religion: Unknown — no public statement of personal faith located (Cuban-American heritage often correlates with Catholic, but unverified in available sources)
profile.background: Marlene Galán-Woods. First-generation Cuban-American (parents fled Castro's Cuba 1960s); raised Brooklyn NY in Spanish-speaking household. Broadcast journalist / investigative reporter in Arizona since 1988 (Fox 10 / Channel 5). Founded Mesa Arts Academy within Boys & Girls Clubs of East Valley. 2022 campaign chair for AZ SoS Adrian Fontes. Widow of Grant Woods (fmr AZ AG 1991-99, R-turned-D, died Oct 2021). Five children incl. youngest adopted from Guatemala. 2024 AZ-01 D primary 3rd place (lost to Amish Shah by <1,800 votes); relaunched March 2025 for 2026 cycle. FEC H6AZ01280. Former Republican; switched citing Trump as 'the last straw.'

CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (noted but no federal-cell credit): 'Strongly opposes any calls to defund the police' — HARD pro-LEO break from progressive lane (federal rubric has no police cell); anti-tariff posture; ACA-incrementalist not M4A. NULL on christian_liberty, economic (no debt-ceiling/BBA statement), Ukraine/China/Iran/foreign-aid (no statements), tariffs (deviation but no federal cell mapped), regulation, industry capture. set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (NEVER FILED — declined AZ-01 bid; endorsed Joseph Chaplik for the seat; pivoted to Scottsdale City Council 2026 race; previously AZ State Sen LD-23 2019-2023, AZ State House 2011-2019, lost 2022 R primary for AZ SoS to Mark Finchem, lost 2024 R primary for Maricopa BoS)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — CANDIDACY CURRENCY (status fix is the win): NEVER FILED for AZ-01. Wikipedia 2026 AZ House elections page lists her as 'Declined'; she endorsed Joseph Chaplik for the AZ-01 seat. Pivoted to Scottsdale City Council 2026 race (8 candidates, 3 seats; Q1 2026 fundraising leader $38,759). Status flipped to former (left federal queue); record retained for institutional history. Modest evidence from her 11-year AZ State Senate/House voting record on issues that map to federal cells: pro-life (Planned Parenthood AZ 0% rating 2012; 'human life begins at conception'); sponsored SB 1485 (2021) Permanent Early Voting List inactive-voter purge (Ducey signed) + mail-in voter ID requirement (2021). NUANCED ELECTION RECORD: pro voter-roll cleanup BUT publicly criticized 2021 Maricopa 'Cyber Ninjas' audit as 'botched'; booed at Trump rally July 2021; Trump endorsed her 2022 SoS primary opponent Mark Finchem (she lost). 2017: ONLY R to vote NO on Blue Lives Matter bill increasing penalties for assaulting off-duty cops — anti-MAGA-aligned independent R. 2018 Don Shooter sexual-harassment accusation led to first MeToo-era state-legislator expulsion.
profile.background: b. June 28 1980, Scottsdale AZ. BA Business Administration, Arizona State University 2003. AZ House LD-8 (2011-2013), AZ House LD-23 (2013-2019), AZ Senate LD-23 (2019-2023). Lost 2022 R primary AZ SoS (to Mark Finchem). Lost 2024 R primary Maricopa County Board of Supervisors. Currently running 2026 Scottsdale City Council. Married Joe Rita.
